cards.back: When a variable in a nested block has the same name as a variable in an outer block, the nested variable hides the outer variable in the nested block's scope.

cards.question: What does this code print?
cards.explanation: The inner block's 'apples' shadows the outer 'apples'. Inside the inner block, it prints 10. After the inner block ends, the outer 'apples' is accessible again and prints 5.

cards.back: No. Avoid shadowing variables as it makes code harder to understand and can lead to subtle bugs. Most compilers will warn about shadowing if warnings are enabled.

cards.text: When a local variable shadows a global variable, you can access the global using {{c1::::}} (the scope resolution operator with no prefix).

cards.front: Can you access an outer local variable that has been shadowed by an inner local variable?
cards.back: No. There is no way to access a shadowed local variable from a nested block. Only shadowed global variables can be accessed using ::.

cards.question: What's the problem with this code?
cards.answer: This prints 5, not 7. The function printValue() uses the global 'value', not the local one from main().
cards.explanation: Shadowing only affects the scope where the shadowing occurs. Functions called from that scope still see the outer variable, not the shadowing one.

cards.front: What compiler warning flag helps detect variable shadowing?
cards.back: -Wshadow (for GCC/Clang). Enable this warning to catch accidental shadowing.

cards.text: To avoid shadowing, use {{c1::different names}} for variables in nested scopes, or limit variable scope so variables in different scopes {{c2::don't overlap}}.

cards.front: Can function parameters shadow global variables?
cards.back: Yes. Function parameters can shadow global variables with the same name. This is common but should be avoided or use 'g_' prefix for globals.

cards.explanation:
Shadowing makes code confusing because:
  • It's not obvious which variable is being used
  • It can lead to logic errors when you think you're using one variable but are using another
  • It makes debugging harder
  • Other developers reading the code may misunderstand which variable is in scope

cards.text: Avoid {{c1::shadowing}} variables as it makes code harder to understand and can lead to {{c2::subtle bugs}}.
Use the {{c1::-Wshadow}} compiler flag to get warnings about variable shadowing.
When a variable in a {{c1::nested block}} has the same name as a variable in an {{c2::outer block}}, the nested variable {{c3::hides}} the outer variable.
